epics.name: Infrastructure Pipeline
epics.description: Server analytics + Pi hardware integration via crawl/walk/run progression. Two CIO-approved specs define the work. Absorbs B-022, B-027, B-014, B-023.

features.title: Server Crawl/Walk/Run Pipeline (Chi-Srv-01)
features.notes: COMPLETE 2026-04-17 â€” 18/18 stories shipped across 3 sprints: Sprint 7 (crawl 9), Sprint 8 (walk 4), Sprint 9 (run 5). Absorbs B-022 (9 US-CMP stories), pipeline MVP (US-147), and B-027 server-side stories. 8 new stories (US-156â€“163). Server code in src/server/ within OBD2v2 repo. Live on chi-srv-01:8000 with Ollama-backed AI analysis, auto-analysis on sync, backup receiver, baseline calibration, AI-enhanced CLI reports.

features.title: Pi Crawl/Walk/Run/Sprint Pipeline (chi-eclipse-01)
features.notes: 23 stories total: 8 crawl (deploy + validate on ARM), 5 walk (sync + WiFi), 4 run (Bluetooth + car install), 6 sprint (real driving + Spool review). Absorbs B-012, B-014, B-023, B-027 Pi-side stories. 12 new stories (US-164-175) + 2 Sprint 11 rough-edge fixes (US-184/185). Hostname: chi-eclipse-01. **Crawl phase COMPLETE 2026-04-18 (Sprint 10, 8/8 shipped).** **Walk phase COMPLETE 2026-04-18 (Sprint 11, 7/7 shipped).** US-165 slipped from Sprint 11 to a later sprint (display advanced tier polish). US-183 pygame HDMI render remains deferred. **Run phase MILESTONE-CLOSED 2026-04-19 (Sprint 13, 4/5 + 1 blocked).** First real Eclipse data ever persisted to local SQLite (149 rows in 60s; ECU witnessed cold→closed-loop transition; LTFT=0.00%).

run.blockedBy: CIO BT pairing with OBDLink LX + car access
run.note: Closed at MILESTONE 2026-04-19 (Session 23) — first real Eclipse OBD-II data persisted to local SQLite. 4/5 formal pass + 1 blocked (US-170 → TD-024, deferred to Sprint 14 as US-192). Engineering deliverables for US-167+US-168 carry to Sprint 14 housekeeping per Ralph inbox 2026-04-19. BL-006 resolved.

harden.note: Sprint 14 Pi Harden (2026-04-19, Session 24) loaded on sprint/pi-harden. 10 stories: TD-023/24/25/26 fixes + data_source column (Spool CR #4) + Spool Data v2 Stories 1+2 (PIDs + drive_id) + US-167/168 engineering carryforward + US-192 US-170 retry + B-044 audit + API_KEY bake-in. 2S + 8M = 18 size-points. CIO directive: full 10-story sprint, high-value first.

features.title: Sprint JSON Validation Script
features.notes: 8-check pre-flight validator for sprint.json. Proposed by Ralph (2026-04-15). ~200 lines, stdlib only. Location: scripts/validate_sprint.py. Wired into /resize_sprint and ralph.sh. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-14-sprint-contract-design.md

features.title: Analytics Excel Export CLI
features.notes: Windows-friendly Python CLI that pulls drives + statistics + realtime via HTTP (X-API-Key) and writes a single multi-sheet .xlsx for offline analysis in Excel. Filters: --start-date/--end-date, --drive-id (repeatable), --device-id, --params, --output. Defaults to basic PID set (Spool Gate 1 or Core 5 â€” grooming Q). Requires new server GET endpoints (drives, drives/{id}/statistics, drives/{id}/realtime) or a batched export endpoint. CIO ask (Session 19). Detail: offices/pm/backlog/B-041-analytics-excel-export-cli.md

features.title: Rename src/pi/obd/ â†’ src/pi/obdii/ (resolve I-014 name collision)
features.notes: Project's src/pi/obd/ package shadows third-party python-OBD under systemd, causing obd.OBD(...) to fail with 'module obd has no attribute OBD' at runtime. Filed Session 20. Mechanical rename (~45 files). Fulfilled by US-187 in Sprint 12 (in-flight as of 2026-04-18). Detail: offices/pm/backlog/B-042-rename-obd-package-to-obdii.md
features.completedBy: Ralph (US-187, Sprint 12 — obd → obdii rename, 77 files)

features.title: Pi Automatic-Sync + Conditional-Shutdown on Power Loss
features.notes: CIO Session 21 behavior spec: when UPS transitions to BATTERY (car off), Pi checks WiFi. If at DeathStarWiFi (home), auto-syncs delta to Chi-Srv-01 then shuts down. If away, skips sync and shuts down (data stays local until next at-home). On car power-up, Pi boots and logs locally until next power loss. Glue layer doesn't exist yet â€” US-184 UPS signal + US-149 SyncClient + US-181 ShutdownHandler are building blocks. Spec Â§2.3 WiFi detection was explicitly deferred to run/sprint. Needs grooming on: grace-period duration, shutdown trigger mechanism (systemctl poweroff vs HAT signal), sync timeout budget, mid-drive BATTERY handling. Proposed 3-story split: US-188 WiFi detection, US-189 PowerLossOrchestrator, US-190 lifecycle test. Not for Sprint 12 â€” Sprint 13+ candidate alongside B-037 Run phase. Detail: offices/pm/backlog/B-043-pi-auto-sync-and-conditional-shutdown.md
features.progressNote: US-188 (WiFi detection, Sprint 13) complete. US-189 (PowerLossOrchestrator) + US-190 (lifecycle test) pending — gated on CIO car-accessory wiring.

features.title: Config-Driven Infrastructure Addresses (Standing Rule)
features.notes: STANDING RULE: infrastructure addresses (IPs, hostnames, ports, base URLs, MAC) MUST live in config â€” never as string literals in source/tests/scripts/deploy/specs. Both Pi and server tiers. CIO directive Session 21. Triggered when chi-srv-01 IP drift (.120 vs .10) broke US-166 e2e validation; underlying bug was 32 files with hardcoded address. Targeted sweep fixed the immediate bug; this story makes addresses config-driven so it can't recur. Phases: (1) audit/categorize literals, (2) centralize in config.json infrastructure section + ConfigValidator + bash consumer pattern, (3) pre-commit lint, (4) document standing rule in specs/standards.md. Sprint 14+ candidate. Standing rule applies NOW for any new code. Detail: offices/pm/backlog/B-044-config-driven-infrastructure-addresses.md

features.title: Replace Physics-Based Simulator With Flat-File Replay
features.notes: STANDING RULE: until real OBD-II data flows from OBDLink LX, 'simulate' = flat-file replay, NOT physics-based generation. CIO directive Session 21 â€” same pattern as Session 19 server-side regression fixtures. Phases: build fixture format (SQLite tape recommended), generate canned scenarios (cold_start, local_loop, errand_day), build replay harness scripts/replay_pi_fixture.sh (stop systemd â†’ SCP fixture â†’ run sync_now.py â†’ assert exact server row counts), rewire validate_pi_to_server.sh to use it, decide eclipse-obd.service default behavior (recommend: drop --simulate flag, service runs idle until BT comes online), deprecate (don't delete) E-03 physics simulator. Triggered by today's failed e2e validation: physics-sim launch hangs SSH, two simulators racing on same SQLite, non-deterministic row counts. Sprint 13 candidate alongside BT pairing work. Detail: offices/pm/backlog/B-045-flat-file-replay-simulator.md

techDebt.title: Config File Drift (Display Dimensions, Data Retention)
techDebt.description: display.width/height (240x240 should be 480x320), dataRetention.realtimeDataDays (7 should be 365). B-020 tracks fix.

techDebt.title: Missing Simulation Output Database Validation Test
techDebt.description: No automated test validates --simulate mode writes all 13 OBD parameters to database. Promoted to B-026.

techDebt.title: OLLAMA_GENERATE_TIMEOUT Not Configurable

techDebt.title: _checkNetworkReachable Placement Design Decision

techDebt.title: Sprint Stories Need Better Stitching

techDebt.title: Post-Reorg Path Drift Cleanup
techDebt.description: Makefile (lines 72,75), README.md (lines 42-43, 173-176), deploy/README.md (line 32), deploy/install-service.sh (lines 106-107, 153), deploy/eclipse-obd.service (line 46), docs/cross-platform-development.md (lines 178, 192, 205), .claude/commands/review-stories-tuner.md (line 49) still reference pre-reorg paths (src/main.py, src/obd_config.json). Mechanical one-PR cleanup. Filed per Ralph's Sweep 6 exit report (2026-04-14). deploy/eclipse-obd.service will actively fail if installed.
